steps.title: Follow the reward chain
steps.content: The passage links rewards to dopamine, dopamine to fan cell activity, and fan cells to the formation of new associative memories. Consequently asks for the result of that chain.

steps.title: Reject distorted outcomes
steps.content:
• Choice A: The passage says rewards produce dopamine, not reduce the ability to use it.
  • Choice B: Associative memory concerns connections between unrelated things, not differences between them.
  • Choice D: The passage discusses fan cell activity, not creation of new fan cells.

steps.title: Connect dopamine to memory
steps.content: If rewards produce dopamine and dopamine supports the cells needed for associative memory, rewards should improve the ability to form those memories. The answer is C.
